entries.title: VPR (Beta) promoted to primary VPR on July 1, 2026
entries.description: The enhanced VPR model, available as VPR (Beta) for the past several months, will become the primary Vulnerability Priority Rating across the entire Tenable platform on July 1. Legacy VPR scoring will be retired. Asset Criticality Ratings (ACR) will also be updated to more accurately reflect real-world business risk as part of an enhanced asset classification engine update.

entries.title: Network Scanner results feed into core risk engine with reachability validation
entries.description: Network Scanner results now feed directly into the core risk engine, dynamically confirming what is actually reachable across AWS, GCP, Azure, and OCI. Instead of flagging every internet-facing asset, the platform surfaces only toxic combinations on truly exposed assets — reducing noise from assets behind a WAF or other controls.

entries.title: Unified accounts page for all cloud and identity accounts
entries.description: A single unified view now consolidates every cloud and identity account across all providers, eliminating provider-specific silos and giving security teams one place to review account status and risk.

entries.title: Container scan noise reduction, Explorer automation rules, and Entra ID / GCP least privilege
entries.description: Three workflow improvements: unresolvable CVEs can now be excluded from container scans to protect dev velocity; any Explorer investigation can be converted into a permanent automation rule for ongoing triage at scale; custom roles for over-privileged Entra ID and GCP groups are now auto-generated based on real usage patterns to enforce least privilege without manual analysis. Updated engine also surfaces vulnerabilities buried in nested JAR files.

entries.title: VM-Native OT Discovery — identify PLCs, HMIs, and IoT devices using existing VM tooling
entries.description: Tenable VM now supports native OT discovery, safely identifying and profiling connected PLCs, HMIs, and IoT devices without specialised hardware or complex deployments. Security teams can turn their existing IT vulnerability management tools into a safe OT discovery engine and gain visibility into the IT/OT security gap using scan templates and discovery settings they already own.

entries.title: New dashboards and reports for Java, database, and OS layer visibility
entries.description: Six new reports and dashboards targeting previously hidden infrastructure risk: Java Visibility and Exposures Dashboard and Report (identifies legacy flaws, library exploits, and unmanaged Java applications); Database Application Visibility and Exposures Dashboard and Report (highlights critical database gaps, unsupported versions, and exploitable assets for compliance and audit); OS and Application Inventory with Data Troubleshooting Report (high-level OS/application inventory with queries to identify and fix scan fidelity issues).

entries.title: Nessus v10.12 — drag-and-drop organisation, OpenSSL 3.5, and FIPS 140-3 support
entries.description: Nessus v10.12 is available for early access with GA expected later this month. New capabilities include drag-and-drop scan organisation into folders from list view, instant .nessus file import by dragging from desktop, full OpenSSL 3.5 support for latest cryptographic standards, and FIPS 140-3 compliance support.

entries.title: Security Center 6.8 — AI-powered VPR insights, unified asset repositories, and VM-native OT Discovery
entries.description: SC 6.8 delivers AI-powered VPR insights with clear mitigation guidance, unified asset repositories for IPv4, IPv6, and Agents in a single view, new background query processing for operational efficiency, and scan optimisation tools. Also includes VM-native OT Discovery, allowing rapid mapping of unknown cyber-physical systems (PLCs, IoT devices) using existing SC tools without additional agents or add-on purchases.

entries.title: OT Security 4.6 — 5,000-subnet scaling, centralized network management, and remote agent updates
entries.description: OT Security 4.6 introduces massive enterprise scaling (up to 5,000 subnets per ICP), a new Monitored Networks page with bulk-add and inactive network staging, per-scan credential customisation for sensitive asset discovery, single-click pivot back to Tenable One for SSO/SAML users, remote OT agent updates from the ICP eliminating manual site visits, and enhanced asset log export metadata for faster diagnostics. IoT connector stability improvements for Milestone, AvigilonES, and Exacq Edge. Note: customers on 4.5 should apply the 4.5.61 service pack immediately.

entries.title: Golden Ticket IoA now directory-aware; richer PetitPotam context; self-healing platform connections
entries.description: Detection improvements: Golden Ticket IoA updated with directory-aware logic to reduce false positives and missed hits in multi-domain environments; PetitPotam detections now surface hostnames and source IPs for faster triage. Platform stability improvements: accurate API pagination for cleaner result set iteration, and self-healing RabbitMQ and Sysvol connections that auto-recover after restarts or network interruptions.

entries.title: PCI ASV interface update — simplified compliance workflow launching May 8, 2026
entries.description: The PCI ASV interface will update on or around May 8, 2026. Changes include renamed actions (Submit PCI → Import to ASV Workbench), a new Accept button and compliance dialog for fewer clicks, a real-time progress indicator, a unified Vulnerability Review & Disputes tab combining Failures and Disputes, and repositioned navigation. No impact to data, scan history, attestation records, or scan configurations — update is automatic.

entries.title: Potential Vulnerabilities introduced — new finding type with lower certainty classification
entries.description: Tenable Research is officially introducing Potential Vulnerabilities as a new finding type: a finding with a lower degree of certainty as to whether the assessed application is or is not vulnerable. Also released this month: improved printer OS fingerprinting (OS artifacts now surface in scan host metadata for identified printers even when Scan Network Printers is disabled), 4,500+ new published vulnerability plugins, and 130+ new compliance audits.

entries.title: Full Export for Attack Path Analysis
entries.description: Full dataset exports for Top Attack Paths and Top Attack Techniques into CSV or JSON formats, supporting up to 100K+ results via a new global UI button. Exports include critical metrics like Source Node Exposure Score (NES) and Target Asset Criticality Rating (ACR), with API parity so teams can pull path data into SIEM, SOAR, or custom tooling via the Tenable Public API.

entries.title: Synchronized Policy Management via Linked Queries
entries.description: Saved explorer searches can now be linked directly to custom policies and reports. When a source query is updated, every linked policy and report automatically syncs — eliminating manual version control and ensuring security standards are identical across the entire organization.

entries.title: Enable/Disable Toggle for Automation Rules
entries.description: New operational control allowing teams to pause automated remediation workflows for maintenance without losing configurations. Prevents accidental changes during planned downtime while keeping automation rules intact and ready to re-enable.

entries.title: CI/CD Pipeline Noise Reduction for Container Scans
entries.description: Unresolvable vulnerabilities can now be excluded from container image scans in CI/CD pipelines, preventing noise from breaking builds when no patch is currently available. Maintains developer velocity without compromising on real, actionable findings.

entries.title: Network Endpoints in Inventory (Confirmed Reachability)
entries.description: Network Endpoints are now displayed in the Inventory, surfacing actual validated entry points for cloud resources. Bridges the gap between theoretical risk and actual exposure by confirming which resources are genuinely reachable from the internet.

entries.title: Terraform Dynamic Configuration Scanning
entries.description: Tenable Cloud Security now scans Terraform dynamic configurations, providing visibility into scaled infrastructure and complex definitions before deployment. Catches IaC misconfigurations that static analysis tools miss in dynamically generated resources.

entries.title: Expanded Compliance: CIS AWS 6.0.0 & NIS2 Directive
entries.description: Immediate support added for CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark 6.0.0 and the NIS2 Directive, keeping cloud accounts aligned with the latest global regulatory standards. Relevant for customers in EU-regulated industries or with AWS compliance obligations.

entries.title: VM-Native OT Discovery
entries.description: Safely identify and profile connected PLCs, HMIs, and IoT devices using existing Tenable VM scan templates — no specialized hardware or new network sensors required. Turns the existing IT security toolset into a safe OT discovery engine, closing the IT/OT visibility gap without a separate product deployment.

entries.title: OS and Application Inventory with Data Troubleshooting Dashboard
entries.description: New dashboard providing an instant high-level view of asset counts across every OS and application, with built-in troubleshooting queries to identify and fix scan fidelity issues. Enables risk prioritization based on accurate, clean data rather than noisy or duplicate scan results.

entries.title: Native OT Discovery Inside Security Center
entries.description: OT discovery capabilities added natively to Tenable Security Center, enabling safe mapping of shadow OT assets — HVAC controllers, IoT devices, PLCs, HMIs — using existing tools. Provides deep identity data for industrial assets without deploying new network sensors or risking operational disruption.

entries.title: Security Center 6.8 — AI-Powered VPR & Unified Asset Repositories
entries.description: Version 6.8 introduces AI-powered VPR insights with clear mitigation guidance, unified asset repositories for IPv4, IPv6, and Agents, and new background query processing and scan optimization tools. Focuses security teams on the vulnerabilities that truly matter while improving operational efficiency at scale.

entries.title: OT Security 4.5.61 Service Pack — Critical Stability Update
entries.description: Critical service pack addressing system stability and performance issues when processing high volumes of network conversations, plus specific fixes for communication gaps with Rockwell Stratix devices and Nessus scans. Immediate upgrade recommended for all customers on version 4.5.

entries.title: OT Security 4.6 Early Access — 5,000 Subnet Scaling & Centralized Network Management
entries.description: Major release introducing support for up to 5,000 subnets per ICP for massive enterprise OT deployments, a new Monitored Networks page with bulk-add capabilities, precision Nessus scan workflows with per-credential control, remote OT agent updates from the ICP, and enhanced diagnostics with deeper asset log metadata. Also includes major stability fixes for Milestone, AvigilonES, and Exacq Edge IoT connector integrations.

entries.title: Secrets Validation — Automatic Live Credential Testing
entries.description: New Secrets Validation capability automatically tests detected tokens — GitHub keys, AI service API keys, and other credentials — to determine if they are live and exploitable. Distinguishes between harmless strings and critical vulnerabilities, enabling prioritization based on real-world risk rather than noise from false positives.

entries.title: Patch Management v10.0.971.26 — Strategy & Compliance Fixes
entries.description: Critical update addressing strategy corruption bugs and inaccurate compliance reporting. Includes modernized performance improvements and security updates via Java 25. Ensures patch workflows remain intact and compliance data stays accurate for reporting and audit purposes.

entries.title: Tenable One Open Connector
entries.description: A new self-service connector allowing teams to ingest data from any external source — pentesting reports, third-party vulnerability scans, custom tools — via drag-and-drop CSV, Excel, or ZIP file uploads with no APIs or coding required. Includes customizable data mapping for precise segmentation and reporting, closing visibility gaps across the full attack surface.

entries.title: AI Exposure — Shadow AI Discovery & Governance
entries.description: Expanded AI Exposure capabilities covering three core areas: continuous shadow AI discovery across the full environment, protection of AI workloads and agents by closing infrastructure and attack path gaps, and a new governance add-on for monitoring employee use of GenAI tools to prevent data leakage and enforce organizational policies.

entries.title: Remediation Patches Integrated into Vulnerability Tables
entries.description: Tenable Plugin IDs and exact patch names are now displayed directly in vulnerability tables and workload profiles. DevOps teams get the precise patch name they need without manual research, significantly reducing Mean Time to Remediation (MTTR). Available in the new Patch Name column and within Patch Profiles.

entries.title: Exclusions Framework for Non-Actionable Findings
entries.description: New Exclusions framework lets teams silence non-actionable findings and focus remediation effort on risks that move the needle. Provides strategic noise reduction without losing visibility into findings that may become relevant later.

entries.title: AWS ABAC Support for True Least Privilege
entries.description: Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) support for AWS brings granular identity visibility and highly accurate permission recommendations. Enables more precise least-privilege enforcement in AWS environments using tag-based access policies.

entries.title: GraphQL API Support for Projects
entries.description: New GraphQL API support for Cloud Security Projects enables security governance to be baked directly into DevOps CI/CD workflows at scale. Allows automated management of project configurations, policies, and findings without manual intervention.

entries.title: Forrester Wave Strong Performer — CNAPP Q1 2026
entries.description: Tenable named a Strong Performer in the Forrester Wave for Cloud Native Application Protection Platforms, Q1 2026. Received perfect 5/5 scores in CIEM, Container Orchestration Protection, Reporting, Vision, and Community. Forrester specifically highlighted excellence in identifying toxic permission combinations and customer support.

entries.title: AI Exposure & MCP Tracking Dashboard
entries.description: Updated Tracking AI Exposure dashboard and report replacing complex plugin output filters with simplified plugin family filters. Adds dedicated content for Model Context Protocol (MCP) to secure AI connectivity alongside LLM deployments, giving security teams a cleaner view of their AI attack surface.

entries.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Analysis Dashboard
entries.description: New Post Quantum Ciphers Analysis report and dashboards help teams inventory their cryptographic landscape and prepare for the transition away from RSA and ECC. Identifies where legacy cryptography is deployed, detects services lacking post-quantum cipher support, and pinpoints specific vulnerable ciphers, certificates, and assets requiring immediate attention.

entries.title: CPU Resource & Plugin Download Concurrency Controls
entries.description: New controls allowing teams to set CPU utilization limits for Windows and Linux agents and throttle plugin download concurrency across agents and scanners. Balances security scan coverage with the performance needs of business-critical infrastructure and bandwidth-constrained networks.

entries.title: Security Center 6.8 — Enhanced VPR with AI-Powered Insights
entries.description: New AI-powered VPR logic explains why an exposure is significant and provides clear mitigation guidance based on regional and industry-specific threat actor behavior. Helps teams stop chasing the 60% of CVEs rated High/Critical and focus on the 3% that truly matter.

entries.title: Security Center 6.8 — Unified Asset Repository & Background Queries
entries.description: IPv4, IPv6, and Agent repositories unified into a single flexible Asset Repository type, reducing administrative overhead. New background query processing keeps the UI responsive during long-running asset searches, and new per-host scan timeouts prevent a single slow host from derailing entire scan schedules.

entries.title: OT Security 4.5 — Advanced Dynamic Tagging & Policy Violation Widgets
entries.description: New rule-based dynamic tags with multiple filters (asset type, risk score, criticality) streamline OT asset prioritization and reporting. New High-Risk Violations and Operational Violations widgets in the Overview Dashboard replace the former Events widgets, making it easier to distinguish critical exposures from non-critical operational issues.

entries.title: OT Security 4.5 — Unified SOC Visibility & IEC 61850 Support
entries.description: Policy violations detected by OT Security (unauthorized access, failed logins, risky config changes) can now be viewed directly within Tenable Security Center dashboards, giving SOC and IT teams a unified OT+IT view. Enhanced passive detection for IEC 61850 intelligent electronic devices improves visibility across substation and power generation infrastructure.

entries.title: OT Security 4.5 — NIST CSF & IEC 62443 Compliance Mapping
entries.description: Direct mapping of asset data and policies to NIST CSF and IEC 62443-3-3 frameworks simplifies compliance tracking and reporting for electrical substation and power grid environments. Includes new RBAC controls in Enterprise Manager allowing admins to assign users to specific ICPs via user groups.

entries.title: Dynamic Objects Misconfiguration IoE
entries.description: New Indicator of Exposure detecting Dynamic Objects Misconfiguration and Usage in Active Directory. Identifies transient AD objects that attackers could exploit for unauthorized access or persistence, hardening environments where dynamic object usage creates stealthy attack paths.

entries.title: Domain Installation Health Check Optimization
entries.description: Improved health checks detect redundant 'Tenable IoA GPO EVT Subscribe Listener' files in SYSVOL, preventing detection lag or GPO conflicts caused by running multiple versions. Ensures the identity detection engine operates at peak performance without configuration drift.

entries.title: WAS Integration for ServiceNow VR v30.2.0
entries.description: Full integration of Tenable Web App Scanning (WAS) with ServiceNow Vulnerability Response v30.2.0, enabling automatic sync of DAST findings and application metadata into ServiceNow. Includes CMDB correlation, scalable chunk-based data ingestion via Export APIs, and flexible CI Lookup/Product Model configuration. Compatible with Zurich, Yokohama, Washington, and Xanadu releases.

entries.title: Tenable Plugin for Jira On-Premises v11.0.0
entries.description: New version adding full support for Jira 11.x Data Center environments, enabling automatic sync of findings from Tenable VM, Security Center, and WAS directly into Jira tickets. Note: not backward compatible with Jira 9.x or 10.x — Jira environment upgrade required.

entries.title: Tenable Add-on for Splunk v8.0.2
entries.description: Quality update resolving a specific index_time race condition that was affecting data reliability for Tenable Security Center integrations. Improves data consistency for teams using Splunk as their SIEM alongside Tenable SC.

entries.title: Native Jira & ServiceNow Ticket Creation
entries.description: Automatic ticket generation from findings directly into Jira Cloud and ServiceNow ITSM. Teams can now close the loop between exposure discovery and remediation without leaving the platform — no manual export, no copy-paste, no workflow gap.

entries.title: Granular RBAC — Read-Only & Custom Exposure Roles
entries.description: New read-only role and a fully customizable Exposure Management role with tag enforcement. Admins can now control exactly who can view, act on, and export data across Tenable One modules — enabling broader stakeholder access without risking data integrity.

entries.title: Editable Dashboard Widgets
entries.description: Users can now edit both built-in and custom dashboard widgets directly — adjusting widget type, categories, values, data labels, stacking, and filters without rebuilding from scratch.

entries.title: IT/OT Unified View Widget
entries.description: New dashboard widget allowing security teams to view IT and OT environments as a single connected surface. Designed to protect uptime and safety by giving IT and OT teams shared visibility from one dashboard.

entries.title: Inventory Export (CSV & JSON)
entries.description: Asset and finding data from the inventory can now be exported directly to CSV or JSON format. Makes it simple to share insights across teams, feed data into reporting tools, or provide evidence for audits — without needing to build a custom integration.

entries.title: Attack Path Analysis — New MITRE ATT&CK Filters
entries.description: Attack paths can now be filtered by MITRE ATT&CK technique ID and 'Archived by User' status, enabling faster and more targeted investigation of specific adversary techniques.

entries.title: Bright DAST Connector
entries.description: New third-party connector for Bright, a DAST tool that pulls asset and findings data from web applications. Configurable directly within the Integrations section of Tenable Exposure Management.

entries.title: Findings Global Search
entries.description: Advanced custom query builder directly on the Findings page — supporting structured filters, operators, properties, and workflow selectors. Eliminates the need to switch pages or export data to run complex queries.

entries.title: Information Order Configuration (Deduplication Control)
entries.description: Administrators can now define which data source takes priority when the same asset appears across multiple connectors. Gives full control over how the asset inventory is built and displayed.

entries.title: ServiceNow Connector Asset Enrichment Options
entries.description: When configuring the ServiceNow third-party connector, users can now configure Asset Enrichment options to pull ServiceNow attributes directly into asset details.

entries.title: 7 New Built-In Dashboard Widgets
entries.description: Seven additional built-in widgets added to the dashboard widget library, giving teams more ways to visualize and analyze exposure data without building custom widgets from scratch.

entries.title: 300+ Validated Third-Party Integrations
entries.description: Tenable One surpassed 300 validated integrations, making it the most open and interconnected exposure management platform on the market. New additions include Claroty xDome (OT) and Bright (DAST).

entries.title: Tag Creation from Global Asset Search
entries.description: Tags can now be created directly from a search query on the Assets page — instantly converting any search result into a dynamic tag without rebuilding filters in the tagging workflow.

entries.title: Asset Details Information Order Display
entries.description: Improved data transparency for deduplicated assets: the sequence of sources used to populate each asset property is now visible, giving teams clarity over where each value came from.

entries.title: 4 New Chart Types & Analytics Enhancements
entries.description: Four new chart types available when creating custom widgets, along with new CISA KEV and end-of-life widget properties. Dashboard data updates now triggered at 3AM per container region timezone.

entries.title: Tenable AI Exposure — General Availability
entries.description: General availability of the AI Exposure module within Tenable One. Unifies AI asset discovery, shadow AI detection, non-human identity governance, and AI risk prioritization in a single platform.

entries.title: Claroty xDome OT Connector
entries.description: New connector for Claroty xDome, pulling OT/ICS asset and findings data from industrial environments directly into Tenable Exposure Management.

entries.title: Attack Path Analysis — AI Assistant Button
entries.description: Added an AI Assistant button in the Attack Path Analysis canvas interaction panel, enabling natural language queries and AI-guided investigation directly within the attack path workflow.

entries.title: Attack Path Analysis — 25% Performance Improvement
entries.description: Data pipeline execution time reduced by 25%. Attack Path Analysis now processes only licensed assets, optimizing performance and reducing data processing overhead for large environments.

entries.title: Cloud Labels as External Tags
entries.description: Cloud labels can now be used to filter, search, and create tags using the new External Tags property. Improves asset context and enables creation of custom exposure cards using cloud labels.

entries.title: Attack Path Quick Filters
entries.description: New quick filter buttons on the Discover page in Attack Path Analysis automatically filter the top attack paths list in one click, significantly reducing time-to-insight during triage.

entries.title: Path Priority Rating Metric
entries.description: Each attack path now has a Path Priority Rating metric, giving users a standardized score to better visualize and compare attack path severity, with drill-down to choke points within each path.

entries.title: Exposure Response — Initiatives
entries.description: New Exposure Response section in Tenable VM. Enables creation of initiatives to track identification of critical exposures and team response, with SLA assignment and vulnerability targeting.

entries.title: Inventory Module — Asset, Tag & Weakness Views
entries.description: Introduction of the new Inventory application with dedicated views for Assets, Tags, and Weaknesses. Includes asset classes, new asset properties, static and dynamic tag creation, and a Weakness Inventory.

entries.title: Advanced Search with NLP
entries.description: New Advanced Search capability supporting both natural language processing queries and a structured query builder leveraging asset classes and properties, with bookmarks and pre-configured Tenable queries.

entries.title: Vulnerability Intelligence Section
entries.description: New Vulnerability Intelligence main section added to Tenable VM, providing enriched context on CVEs including threat intelligence, exploit availability, and real-world exploitation evidence.

entries.title: Configurable Plugin Update Plans
entries.description: New Configure Plugin Update Plan setting in Agent Profiles. Teams can now auto-update, delay plugin updates by 1–30 days, or pin to a specific delayed set — enabling testing environments to validate plugins before production rollout.

entries.title: API Access Security — Source IP Restriction
entries.description: Tenable VM now supports source IP address restrictions for public API access. Administrators can allowlist specific IPv4/IPv6 addresses, ranges, or CIDR blocks for extra API authentication.

entries.title: Redesigned License Information Page
entries.description: Redesigned License Information page providing visual overviews by product and time period, license usage snapshots, resource counts for all cloud products, and account information including Customer ID.
